# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that creates releases and publishes packages. On version tags, build the application, create a GitHub release, and publish to npm/PyPI. Make the release process fully automated so developers can just push tags to trigger releases.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD

on:
  push:
    tags: true

jobs:
  build_and_release:
    name: Build and Release
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v2

    - name: Set up Node.js
      uses: actions/setup-node@v2
      with:
        node-version: 14

    - name: Install and build
      run: |
        npm ci
        npm run build

    - name: Publish to npm
      uses: npm/node-action@v1
      with:
        registry-auth: ${{ secrets.NPM_AUTH_TOKEN }}
        argument: -registry https://registry.npmjs.org/ --access token ${{ secrets.NPM_AUTH_TOKEN }} publish

    - name: Set up Python environment
      uses: actions/setup-python@v2
      with:
        python-version: 3.8

    - name: Install dependencies and build
      run: |
        pip install -r requirements.txt
        python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel

    - name: Publish to PyPI
      uses: apache/maven@v1
      with:
        maven: '3.6.3'
        arguments: -Darguments='-U -s https://upload.pypi.org/legacy/ --username ${{ secrets.PYPI_USERNAME }} --password ${{ secrets secrets.PYPI_PASSWORD }} upload dist/*'

    - name: Create GitHub release
      uses: actions/create-release@v1
      with:
        tag_name: ${{ github.ref }}
        release_name: ${{ github.ref }}
        draft: false
        prerelease: ${{ github.event.pull_request == null }}
